object_identification.name: blue pen
object_identification.attributes: A slim cylindrical blue plastic writing instrument with a pointed tip and cap-like end, lying on a flat wooden table; lightweight, narrow, and easy to slip if grasped too close to the smooth body.

grasp.contact_zone: around the pen barrel near its center, avoiding the tip and the cap end
grasp.accessibility: high; the pen is isolated on an open tabletop with clear approach space from above and from the side

planning.approach: Move the gripper above the pen along the shortest unobstructed path while keeping the pen centered in the camera view and aligning with its long axis.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Orient the gripper fingers parallel to the pen axis and open slightly wider than the pen diameter before descent.
planning.grasp_contact: Close the fingers gently onto the middle barrel with enough force to prevent slipping but not deform the plastic, ensuring the tip is not pinched.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift vertically a small distance, then hold steady and recheck alignment to avoid rotation or dropping due to the pen's smooth surface and low mass.

use.description: Writing notes or filling forms
use.link_to_grasp: The precision pinch grasp positions the pen for controlled finger motion and stable tip placement on paper.
use.physical_constraints: Requires the tip to remain unobstructed and the barrel to be held lightly enough for fine control.

use.description: Marking or labeling objects
use.link_to_grasp: A centered grasp allows the robot to carry and orient the pen tip downward for accurate marking.
use.physical_constraints: Needs stable wrist orientation and minimal slip, since the pen is thin and smooth.

robustness.assumptions: The pen is rigid, intact, and resting on a flat surface with no strong external disturbances.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If the exact center is uncertain, grasp slightly closer to the pen's middle third and use visual feedback during closure to confirm contact before lifting.

object_identification.name: Blue ballpoint pen
object_identification.attributes: Translucent blue plastic body, thin cylindrical shape, capped tip, resting flat on a smooth surface

grasp.contact_zone: The barrel, avoiding the very tip and the cap
grasp.accessibility: High, unobstructed access from above

planning.approach: Approach from above along the Z-axis, aligned vertically with the pen's center line
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Hand fingers open, approaching perpendicular to the pen's long axis
planning.grasp_contact: Fingers and thumb pinch the barrel firmly
planning.lift_stabilization: Maintain a slight overhang and use wrist rotation if picking up an angled object to counter torque

use.description: Write on paper or a document
use.link_to_grasp: A standard lateral pinch provides the necessary torque control for writing motions
use.physical_constraints: Requires holding the cap off the nib end to expose the writing point

use.description: Mark or highlight text on a screen or wall
use.link_to_grasp: The grip allows for both translational and rotational movement needed for drawing or underlining
use.physical_constraints: Needs enough counter-force applied to prevent the pen from rolling or slipping off the surface

robustness.assumptions: The pen is not capped (or the cap is on), the pen is vertical or nearly so, and it isn't under anything heavy
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Use a camera-based controller to verify grab success before full lift; adjust gripper width if the pen's thickness varies

object_identification.name: blue retractable-tip pen
object_identification.attributes: cylindrical, semi-transparent plastic barrel with visible inner tube, rubber textured grip zone near tip, click button at tail end, pocket clip, lies flat on wood grain table.

grasp.contact_zone: The central body of the pen barrel, or preferably the textured rubber grip area.
grasp.accessibility: High. The object is isolated on a flat surface with no close obstacles, allowing approaches from above or either side.

planning.approach: Top-down approach. The end-effector should move over the pen centerpiece, orienting its fingers perpendicular to the pen's primary axis.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Rotate the robotic wrist so that the two opposing fingers will make contact on the horizontal diameter of the circular cross-section, near its estimated center of mass.
planning.grasp_contact: Simultaneously actuate the fingers to apply equal, opposite force. The gripper pads (rubber or high-friction) should conform slightly to the cylindrical surface.
planning.lift_stabilization: Once contact is confirmed via force sensors, apply a pre-set squeeze force. Lift vertically 5-10cm initially to check for slippage or rotation before further translation.

use.description: writing on paper surface
use.link_to_grasp: A specialized tool-use grasp is needed. The robot should grasp higher up then, potentially, pick up from one end, reorient and regrasp via a fixture or other arm into a pen-hold pose.
use.physical_constraints: High dexterity needed; requires applying specific downward pressure while performing precise movements; tip must be clicked open first.

use.description: inserting into an office organizer hole
use.link_to_grasp: Pick up by the end that will remain *outside* the destination; for example, pick near the tail button.
use.physical_constraints: High positional accuracy and collision avoidance are critical for alignment with the target hole.

robustness.assumptions: The table surface provides sufficient frictional support to prevent rolling during approach/contact; the pen's weight is balanced around the visual center.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: In case of detection error, slightly over-opening the fingers and using a visually-guided tactile search spiral at the estimated location can ensure a successful find. A slight slip can be compensated by increasing grasp force until a maximum threshold.

object_identification.name: ballpoint pen
object_identification.attributes: smooth cylindrical body, blue ink, metal tip, length approximately 14-15cm, lightweight, non-slip grip

grasp.contact_zone: finger pads (index and thumb) around the midsection of the pen
grasp.accessibility: high, accessible from any side due to symmetrical shape

planning.approach: vertical descent aligned with pen axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: pen cap side facing downward for easier grip
planning.grasp_contact: index finger and thumb contact at 1-2 cm from the tip, wrapping partially around the pen
planning.lift_stabilization: gentle upward pull with slight rotational torque to prevent rolling

use.description: writing notes or annotations on paper
use.link_to_grasp: secure grip ensures controlled pen movement for precise writing
use.physical_constraints: ink must not dry out, paper must be flat and stable

use.description: marking or labeling items for organization
use.link_to_grasp: steady grasp allows clear and consistent marking lines
use.physical_constraints: surface must be smooth and free of debris for clean marking

robustness.assumptions: pen is in upright position, no ink leakage, surface is flat
robustness.uncertainty_handling: force control for delicate grip, visual feedback for alignment, adaptive grip pressure

object_identification.name: blue retractable ballpoint pen
object_identification.attributes: cylindrical blue plastic body, retractable writing tip mechanism, small handheld size, unobstructed placement on a flat wooden tabletop

grasp.contact_zone: midsection of the pen's body, between the clicker end and the writing tip end
grasp.accessibility: fully accessible, no surrounding obstacles blocking the pen, approachable from all horizontal directions above the table

planning.approach: move the robotic end-effector directly above the pen, aligned parallel to the pen's long axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: orient the end-effector's pinchers to close around the pen's midsection, with the pinch axis perpendicular to the pen's length
planning.grasp_contact: lower the end-effector to make light contact with the top and bottom sides of the pen's midsection, then close the pinchers with controlled force to secure the pen without damaging its body
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the pen 10-15cm above the table, maintain a steady grip to prevent rotation or slippage, confirm secure hold via visual feedback before further movement

use.description: Write or draw on a flat sheet of paper
use.link_to_grasp: retain the same precision pinch grasp, reorient the pen to point the writing tip downward to contact the paper's surface, adjust grip position to enable natural writing movement
use.physical_constraints: must maintain sufficient grip force to control pen movement across paper, extend the writing tip via the clicker before use, avoid excessive force that tears paper or damages the pen tip

use.description: Store the pen in a desk pen holder for organization
use.link_to_grasp: keep the precision pinch grasp, align the pen's long axis with the open top of the pen holder, lower the pen into the holder to deposit it
use.physical_constraints: must align the pen correctly with the holder's opening to avoid knocking the holder over, release the grasp only after the pen is fully positioned inside the holder to prevent dropping damage

robustness.assumptions: the pen is stationary on the table, its surface is non-slippery, the end-effector has enough precision to execute a small pinch grasp, no unforeseen obstacles block access to the pen
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if the initial grasp fails to secure the pen, reposition the end-effector 5mm from the original contact point and retry; use onboard camera visual feedback to confirm the pen's position and orientation at every step to correct misalignment

object_identification.name: grey mug
object_identification.attributes: A cylindrical grey ceramic mug with a side handle, open top, and visible liquid residue inside; medium size, tabletop resting, likely suitable for hot beverages.

grasp.contact_zone: The mug handle is the primary contact zone; optionally a supporting finger or palm contact on the mug body is avoided to reduce slip and heat exposure.
grasp.accessibility: The handle is clearly visible and accessible from the left side of the mug in the image, with enough clearance for a robot gripper to approach from the side without contacting the rim.

planning.approach: Approach from the side of the handle with the gripper aligned to the handle opening, keeping the wrist level and avoiding the mug rim and liquid surface.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Open gripper with jaws parallel to the handle thickness and slightly angled to center on the handle loop before insertion.
planning.grasp_contact: Close the gripper until both fingers contact the outer and inner surfaces of the handle, applying firm but gentle force to secure the mug without deforming or tipping it.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift vertically a short distance while keeping the mug upright, then stabilize by minimizing acceleration and checking for slip before moving away.

use.description: Drink or serve a hot beverage
use.link_to_grasp: The handle grasp keeps the mug upright and allows safe transport to a person or machine.
use.physical_constraints: Must avoid tilting to prevent spill; grip force must be enough to resist liquid slosh and the mug may be warm.

use.description: Carry or relocate the mug on a tabletop or tray
use.link_to_grasp: A stable handle grasp enables controlled lifting and placement in a new location.
use.physical_constraints: Center of mass shifts with liquid level; motion should be smooth and clearance must be maintained around the rim and handle.

robustness.assumptions: The mug is rigid, intact, and the handle is strong enough for a standard robotic pinch grasp; the surface is dry enough to avoid excessive slip.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If handle pose is uncertain, re-estimate its geometry from a closer view, approach slowly, and use tactile feedback to confirm secure contact before lifting.

object_identification.name: grey mug
object_identification.attributes: material ceramic, cylindrical, handle on side, dark grey color, sitting on wooden table

grasp.contact_zone: handle
grasp.accessibility: high

planning.approach: robot arm position above handle
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: parallel to handle axis, angled down
planning.grasp_contact: close the jaw on handle
planning.lift_stabilization: lift slowly, verify stability before moving

use.description: drink water or coffee
use.link_to_grasp: grasping handle allows secure holding while drinking
use.physical_constraints: mug must be filled, handle must be accessible

use.description: place on table for storage
use.link_to_grasp: grasping handle allows precise placement
use.physical_constraints: surface must be flat and level

robustness.assumptions: mug is stable on table, handle is not broken, no other objects interfere
robustness.uncertainty_handling: use slow lift, verify stability mid-air, adjust grip if needed

object_identification.name: grey mug
object_identification.attributes: cylindrical body, single curved handle, ceramic material

grasp.type: power grasp for handle, or top-down pinch on rim
grasp.contact_zone: The outer surface of the cylindrical body and the inside of the C-shaped handle.
grasp.accessibility: High. Clear access from the top and sides, with the handle offering a stable connection point.

planning.approach: Top-down approach to align with the mug's opening and identify handle position. Horizontal side approach to engage the handle.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Rotate end-effector so that pairs of fingers can straddle the mug's body and one or more fingers can hook inside the handle.
planning.grasp_contact: Simultaneously close gripper digits around the handle and body to secure a firm, multi-point contact.
planning.lift_stabilization: Maintain perpendicularity of the mug's central axis relative to the ground to keep its potential liquid contents level.

use.description: contain and serve beverages
use.link_to_grasp: Secure grasp on the handle and body allows lifting, tilting to pour or drink from, and placing without spilling.
use.physical_constraints: Grasp must be robust to varying weight of contents and resist slippage due to liquid on the surface.

use.description: reheating liquid in a microwave
use.link_to_grasp: Grasp on the body or specifically the handle avoids heated areas, enabling precise insertion and removal.
use.physical_constraints: Limited visibility within the microwave and potential constraints on hand placement make identifying the handle critical for safe removal.

robustness.assumptions: The object is upright and stable on a flat surface; the handle is accessible and strong enough to support the mug's full weight.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Incorporate local tactile feedback to detect slippage and adjust grasp force. Perform visual re-sampling or use probabilistic models to handle occlusions or poor lighting that might obscure the handle location. Execute small 'probing' motions to confirm handle stability before lifting.

object_identification.name: ceramic mug
object_identification.attributes: smooth black ceramic, cylindrical body with a rounded top and bottom, handle on the right side, empty, height ~12cm, diameter ~7cm

grasp.contact_zone: top rim and handle
grasp.accessibility: front-facing, handle accessible from above

planning.approach: vertical descent from above at 45-degree angle toward mug's center
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: fingers aligned parallel to mug's handle, thumb positioned for rim contact
planning.grasp_contact: fingers wrap around handle, thumb presses against top rim (3-point contact)
planning.lift_stabilization: gentle upward force with slight rotational torque to prevent slipping

use.description: drinking hot or cold beverages
use.link_to_grasp: handle grasp ensures stable pouring/spilling prevention
use.physical_constraints: must be empty to prevent spills; weight limit ~0.3kg

use.description: decorative object or small plant holder
use.link_to_grasp: handle grasp allows reorientation for aesthetic placement
use.physical_constraints: must support additional weight (<0.5kg) if used as a holder

robustness.assumptions: surface is flat and stable; mug is empty; no liquid residue on exterior
robustness.uncertainty_handling: use force control to adjust grip strength; visual feedback for rim alignment

object_identification.name: grey ceramic mug
object_identification.attributes: glossy dark grey ceramic body, C-shaped side handle, slightly tapered cylindrical shape, interior with residual light brown liquid, minor residue stains on inner rim, rests on a flat wooden desk surface

grasp.contact_zone: robotic gripper fingers wrap around the mug's C-shaped handle, with one finger on the inner curve of the handle and the other on the outer curve, no contact with the mug's open top
grasp.accessibility: the mug's handle is fully unobstructed, with no surrounding objects blocking access to the handle or the mug's body, clear line of sight for the robotic perception system

planning.approach: robotic arm approaches the mug from the side of its handle, aligning to avoid crossing over the mug's open top to reduce spill risk
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper is oriented horizontally, with its opening aligned to fit around the mug's handle, positioned 5cm away from the handle to prepare for controlled closing
planning.grasp_contact: gripper closes with gentle, consistent force to secure the handle, verifying firm contact that does not shift the mug's base on the desk before initiating a lift
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the mug straight up 10cm above the desk first to confirm the grip is stable, keep the mug vertically oriented during all movement to prevent sloshing or spilling residual liquid

use.description: Transport the mug to a sink for cleaning and rinsing
use.link_to_grasp: the same handle grasp is maintained throughout transport, keeping the mug's top unobstructed for emptying and cleaning at the sink
use.physical_constraints: must keep the mug vertically oriented to contain residual liquid, gripper force limited to avoid cracking the ceramic handle, movement speed restricted to prevent spills

use.description: Deliver the mug with remaining drink to a person for consumption
use.link_to_grasp: the handle-only grasp leaves the mug's opening fully accessible, so a person can drink directly from the mug without the gripper blocking access
use.physical_constraints: maintain vertical orientation to avoid spilling liquid, move at slow consistent speeds to prevent sloshing, avoid positioning the gripper between a person and the mug's opening

robustness.assumptions: the mug's handle is structurally intact to support the full weight of the mug, the desk is flat so the mug is stable pre-grasp, residual liquid volume is low enough to avoid spilling if moved carefully
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if the handle is not detected by perception systems, switch to a lower-body grasp around the base third of the mug to lift safely; if liquid movement is detected, pause movement to stabilize the mug before continuing transport

object_identification.name: computer mouse
object_identification.attributes: Small black wired Lenovo mouse with a red scroll wheel/button area, low-profile rounded body, USB cable attached, and compact form factor suitable for desktop use

grasp.contact_zone: Upper shell on the left and right sides of the mouse body, avoiding the scroll wheel and the cable exit near the front
grasp.accessibility: High accessibility from above; the mouse is isolated on a flat table, but the cable may require avoidance or light repositioning

planning.approach: Move the gripper from above the mouse along a vertical or slightly angled descent while keeping clear of the cable loops
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the gripper parallel to the mouse long axis, with fingers open wider than the mouse width and the palm centered over the body
planning.grasp_contact: Close fingers gently onto the left and right side walls of the mouse body with enough force to secure it without pressing the scroll wheel or deforming the cable
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift slowly while maintaining a vertical hold, then stabilize by keeping the cable slack or supporting the cable separately to prevent torque or swing

use.description: Use as a pointing and selection device for controlling a computer cursor
use.link_to_grasp: The robot must hold the mouse steady enough for precise handoff or placement near a workstation
use.physical_constraints: Cable routing must remain free, and the mouse must rest on a flat surface for optical tracking and smooth sliding

use.description: Use as an input device for clicking, dragging, and menu navigation in desktop tasks
use.link_to_grasp: The grasp should preserve access to the top buttons and scroll wheel for normal operation after placement
use.physical_constraints: The device must remain oriented upright with the cable not twisted or trapped under the body

robustness.assumptions: The mouse is lightweight, rigid, and can be grasped safely from the top without activating buttons unintentionally
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If cable entanglement or occlusion is uncertain, the robot should first clear the cable loop, then use a slower compliant grasp with reduced force and visual feedback

object_identification.name: computer mouse
object_identification.attributes: wireless black model, compact, cable management loop, red sensor light

grasp.type: enclosing grasp, specifically a lateral pinch-to-wrap
grasp.contact_zone: flanks of mouse palm area and side buttons
grasp.accessibility: highly accessible side profile

planning.approach: approach from the right side while the mouse remains in a neutral resting pose
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: align fingers parallel to the top surface to facilitate lifting
planning.grasp_contact: fingers wrap around left and right flanks to include the mouse body contour
planning.lift_stabilization: lift vertically and rotate wrist to align gravitational force with principal inertia axis

use.description: perform cursor navigation on a desktop interface
use.link_to_grasp: requires holding the mouse firmly to control direction via motion
use.physical_constraints: must maintain grip while sensing subtle motor movements

use.description: click and select menu items on a digital interface
use.link_to_grasp: requires steady holding in hand to press left click or scroll wheel
use.physical_constraints: precision movements while sustained grip

robustness.assumptions: mouse is solid, rigid plastic with uniform mass distribution
robustness.uncertainty_handling: err on the side of a more forceful grasp and use multiple sensory modalities to confirm object stability

object_identification.name: Wired Lenovo computer mouse
object_identification.attributes: Standard ergonomic shape, dark grey plastic, two main buttons (left/right), a red scroll wheel, a connected black cable, and a USB connector. The brand 'Lenovo' is partially visible.

grasp.contact_zone: Large curved surfaces on the left and right sides of the main mouse body.
grasp.accessibility: Primarily accessible from the top. Sides and rear are clear. The thin cord presents a potential entanglement risk requiring visual cord tracking.

planning.approach: Direct vertical descent from above, centered on the main mouse body, avoiding collision with the table surface or the cable loop.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the end-effector axis perpendicular to the long axis of the mouse, with the jaws opened to slightly wider than the mouse width.
planning.grasp_contact: Simpultaneous closing and contact of both jaws on opposite curved side walls of the mouse. Maintain sufficient force to overcome slip, avoiding excess force on the thin plastic.
planning.lift_stabilization: Vertical lift at a constant speed to maintain force balance, followed by an accelerometer check for excessive vibrational force from cable swing.

use.description: Operating the computer cursor
use.link_to_grasp: This grasp configuration is unsuitable for functional use. After grasping, the robot must reposition the mouse to lay flat on a surface. A dynamic or multi-fingered hand could re-orient. A parallel gripper would likely need to place it down and re-grasp part from an intended use configuration, or the gripper would need to approach from the back to 'push' or 'hold' it for use.
use.physical_constraints: Must maintain surface contact for optical sensor function. Restricted movement range based on the slack of the USB cable.

use.description: Packaging for shipping
use.link_to_grasp: The described side-wall grasp is ideal for relocating the mouse body into a box. A secondary manipulation step is required to coyl and secure the cable.
use.physical_constraints: Requires precise positioning within a container without damaging the scroll wheel or cable attachment point.

robustness.assumptions: Assumed object friction coefficient is standard for plastic (approx 0.3-0.5). Surrounding surface is perceived as a 'simple flat table'.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Implement a visual servoing step to re-adjust the gripper's target frame relative to the cable pose just before contact to ensure non-collision with the cable. Active force feedback will detect table contact during descent to adjust final contact height.

object_identification.name: Lenovo wired USB computer mouse
object_identification.attributes: Black matte plastic body, central red scroll wheel, attached 1.5m black USB cable, compact ergonomic desktop shape, left/right click buttons integrated into the top surface

grasp.contact_zone: Left and right lateral edges of the mouse body, avoiding the top button/scroll wheel areas to prevent accidental input
grasp.accessibility: Fully accessible on an open wooden table, no surrounding obstacles block access to the mouse's sides

planning.approach: Move the robotic end-effector vertically downward from 30cm above the mouse, aligned with the mouse's long axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: End-effector gripper jaws positioned parallel to the mouse's left and right sides, 5cm above the table surface, with enough spacing to wrap around the mouse's width
planning.grasp_contact: Gripper jaws close gently on the mouse's lateral sides, applying 8N of grip force to secure the mouse without damaging its plastic body
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift the mouse 15cm vertically upward, maintaining a level orientation to keep the attached cable from snagging on the table or pulling the mouse loose from the grasp

use.description: Plug the mouse into a desktop computer's USB port to enable cursor control for computing tasks
use.link_to_grasp: The same precision pinch grasp is maintained to transport the mouse and its cable to the computer's USB port, adjusting grip position if needed to guide the USB connector into the port
use.physical_constraints: The cable must be untangled during movement, the port location must be within the cable's length, and the grip must avoid slippage while inserting the USB connector

use.description: Reposition the mouse to a clear, unobstructed spot on the desk to free up space for a laptop or notebook
use.link_to_grasp: The original grasp is used to lift and carry the mouse, with a secondary adjustment to straighten the cable mid-transit to avoid tangling
use.physical_constraints: The new placement spot must be flat and stable, the cable must be arranged to not hang off the desk edge after placement, and the mouse must be set down without dropping it

robustness.assumptions: The mouse is the only small target object in the workspace, the table surface is flat, the mouse's sides provide sufficient friction for the gripper to hold securely
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If the initial grasp slips, the gripper will increase grip force by 10% and reposition 0.5cm inward to re-grip the mouse; if the cable is tangled, the end-effector will first straighten the cable before grasping the mouse to avoid snags during lifting

object_identification.name: orange latex balloon
object_identification.attributes: Small deflated orange balloon made of thin elastic latex, lightweight, highly deformable, smooth surface, with an open neck and wrinkled body

grasp.type: gentle pinch grasp at the neck
grasp.contact_zone: the reinforced neck/opening area near the balloon mouth, avoiding the thin inflated body
grasp.accessibility: high; the object is isolated on a flat tabletop and can be approached from above with minimal obstruction

planning.approach: move the end effector above the balloon with a slow vertical descent and visual alignment to the neck
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: fingers or suction/pinch tool aligned parallel to the tabletop and centered on the neck opening
planning.grasp_contact: close gently on the neck region using low force to avoid tearing or slipping; avoid squeezing the balloon body
planning.lift_stabilization: lift slowly with minimal acceleration, maintaining a light hold on the neck and checking for slippage due to the smooth, flexible latex

use.description: Use as a party decoration when inflated and tied
use.link_to_grasp: the robot must grasp the neck securely to transfer it to an inflator or tying station
use.physical_constraints: thin latex can tear, so grip force must be low and contact must stay on the neck

use.description: Use as a simple educational prop for demonstrating inflation, elasticity, or robotics picking of deformable objects
use.link_to_grasp: the robot picks it by the neck and places it into a demonstration area or fixture
use.physical_constraints: object is highly deformable and unstable when moved, requiring careful motion planning

robustness.assumptions: the balloon is deflated, intact, and lying freely on a clean flat surface
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if the neck orientation is unclear, use vision to re-center on the opening and apply a compliant, low-force grasp with slip detection and immediate release if excessive deformation or loss of purchase is observed

object_identification.name: Orange Balloon
object_identification.attributes: Uninflated, folded rubber/latex, matte finish

grasp.contact_zone: Balloon neck region
grasp.accessibility: High visibility and approach vector

planning.approach: Vertical descent from above
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Fingers parallel to table surface
planning.grasp_contact: Pinch around the narrow neck
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift gently to check material integrity

use.description: Decorative purpose, inflating for a party
use.link_to_grasp: Requires holding neck anchor point
use.physical_constraints: Must avoid overstretching thin latex

use.description: Tossing as a soft ball toy
use.link_to_grasp: Requires securing the narrow neck
use.physical_constraints: Soft material prone to deformation

robustness.assumptions: Surface is horizontal, object is non-inflated
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Adjust closing force based on material deformation

object_identification.name: uninflated orange balloon
object_identification.attributes: small, flexible, latex material, highly deformable, light orange color, round neck opening clearly visible

grasp.contact_zone: the folded or thickest part of the deflation balloon body
grasp.accessibility: high, lying flat on a table with no surrounding obstacles

planning.approach: vertical down approach (top-down)
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: aligning standard parallel jaw gripper fingers to be parallel with the long axis of the balloon, or perpendicular to the neck for simple acquisition
planning.grasp_contact: lowering gripper to slightly compress the material against the table surface before closing jaws to ensure full acquisition within the fingertips
planning.lift_stabilization: gentle lifting with minimal acceleration to account for the object's extremely low mass and flexibility, relying on friction more than form closure

use.description: placement into a sorting bin for a generic pickup task
use.link_to_grasp: transporting after a pinch grasp on the body
use.physical_constraints: high flexibility makes it difficult to place precisely or maintain a specific internal shape, but easy to drop into a larger container

use.description: providing to a human collaborator or insertion into a machine for inflation
use.link_to_grasp: grasping near the neck (but not covering the opening) might be suitable for handoff or insertion tasks
use.physical_constraints: the neck is soft and floppy, requiring precise relative positioning sensors or compliant insertion strategies

robustness.assumptions: the balloon is initially stationary on a non-slip surface and will conform somewhat to the gripper fingers
robustness.uncertainty_handling: due to the high deformability, success cannot be entirely predicted from object shape alone. The use of a simple pinch is robust against pose orientation errors, but sensor feedback (e.g., tactile or visual check upon lifting) would be required to confirm transport

object_identification.name: orange latex balloon
object_identification.attributes: smooth surface, flexible, inflatable, hollow, irregular teardrop shape, attached by a small neck to a surface

grasp.contact_zone: neck attachment point and lower curved surface
grasp.accessibility: limited by flexibility and surface adhesion

planning.approach: top-down alignment with vertical descent
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: fingers oriented parallel to balloon surface, slight tilt to counter flexibility
planning.grasp_contact: thumb and index finger pinch neck firmly while middle finger stabilizes lower curve
planning.lift_stabilization: gentle upward force with rotational counterbalance to avoid balloon deformation

use.description: child's decorative balloon for party or celebration
use.link_to_grasp: firm pinch on neck allows controlled inflation/deflation and transport
use.physical_constraints: avoid sharp edges during inflation, prevent overinflation to maintain structural integrity

use.description: educational tool for demonstrating buoyancy or pressure principles
use.link_to_grasp: secure grasp enables safe handling during experiments requiring controlled movement
use.physical_constraints: minimize contact pressure to prevent balloon rupture, use soft gripper surfaces

robustness.assumptions: balloon is fully inflated but not overinflated, surface attachment is secure but not adhesive
robustness.uncertainty_handling: force control with adaptive feedback to compensate for surface friction variability and material elasticity

object_identification.name: Deflated orange latex party balloon
object_identification.attributes: Bright orange color, flexible soft rubber latex material, deflated state, elongated shape with a narrow open neck, folded flattened body, lightweight

grasp.contact_zone: Middle section of the balloon's narrow neck, between the open end and the folded main body
grasp.accessibility: Fully unobstructed, placed on an open flat tabletop with clear line of sight and approach access from all standard robot positions

planning.approach: Move the robot end-effector straight down towards the balloon from a top-down orientation to align with the object's long axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the gripper's pinch axis parallel to the balloon's length to avoid crumpling the folded body, position gripper jaws around the narrow neck
planning.grasp_contact: Close the gripper to apply light, even pressure on the neck, sufficient to hold the lightweight balloon without puncturing or tearing the thin latex material
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift the balloon slowly and levelly straight upwards, maintain consistent light grip force to prevent the smooth latex from slipping out of the gripper

use.description: Inflate the balloon for event or party decoration
use.link_to_grasp: Retain the neck pinch grasp, align the balloon's open neck over an air pump nozzle to secure it before initiating inflation
use.physical_constraints: Grip pressure must remain low enough to avoid puncturing latex, the neck can only stretch to fit pump nozzles under 2cm in diameter to prevent tearing

use.description: Use the deflated balloon as a non-slip grip cover for small handheld tools
use.link_to_grasp: After lifting the balloon, stretch its open neck to slide over the handle of a small slippery tool, then re-grasp the tool to use the balloon as a cushioned grip
use.physical_constraints: Tool handle diameter must be under 2cm to avoid tearing the latex neck, the balloon's soft material can only support light tool weights to stay in place

robustness.assumptions: The balloon is intact with no pre-existing punctures, the table surface is stable and flat, the robot's gripper can regulate force to avoid damaging soft materials
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If initial grasp slips due to smooth latex, reposition the gripper to grasp the thicker folded main body for more surface contact, or add soft friction pads to gripper jaws to improve hold; if the balloon is punctured, adjust grip force to lift the lightweight damaged material without further tearing

object_identification.name: blue energy drink can
object_identification.attributes: Small cylindrical aluminum beverage can, blue label, pull-tab top, standing upright on a table, likely lightweight and graspable with one hand

grasp.contact_zone: Lateral surface around the mid-body of the can, avoiding the top pull-tab and bottom rim
grasp.accessibility: High; the can is isolated on a flat table with clear side access and no immediate obstructions

planning.approach: Move the gripper to the side of the can with a straight or slightly angled approach from above and laterally align with the can’s centerline
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Open parallel-jaw gripper with jaws vertical, centered at mid-height of the can, with finger spacing slightly larger than the can diameter
planning.grasp_contact: Close the jaws gently until both sides contact the can evenly with enough normal force to prevent slip but not deform the thin aluminum wall
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift vertically while maintaining symmetric grip and keep the can upright to avoid tipping, rotating, or spilling if opened

use.description: Transport the drink to a user or serving area
use.link_to_grasp: The secure side grasp supports stable lifting and carrying without contacting the top opening
use.physical_constraints: Must keep the can upright and limit acceleration to avoid slippage or liquid motion

use.description: Place the can into a cup holder, tray, or recycling bin
use.link_to_grasp: The cylindrical grasp allows controlled positioning and release in a narrow target location
use.physical_constraints: Requires precise alignment with the target opening and careful release to avoid bouncing or toppling

robustness.assumptions: The can is unopened, rigid enough to support a standard side grasp, and the table surface provides stable support
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If the exact diameter or pose is uncertain, use vision-based centering, approach slowly, and apply a compliant low-force grip with slip detection

object_identification.name: Red Bull Blue energy drink can
object_identification.attributes: 250ml aluminum cylinder, blue color scheme, metallic reflective surface, cylindrical shape

grasp.contact_zone: Vertical side circumference, typically centered along the height of the can or slightly lower third for stability
grasp.accessibility: Highly accessible given the smooth surface and lack of textured grips required for finger precision

planning.approach: Vertical approach from above, aligning the end-effector with the can's central axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: End-effector oriented perpendicular to the table surface, parallel to each other for closure
planning.grasp_contact: Fingers apply inward pressure around the curved lateral surface, ensuring uniform force distribution
planning.lift_stabilization: Maintain vertical orientation during lift by using gripper width to counteract rotational torque; avoid pivoting the can

use.description: Open the can to consume the energy drink
use.link_to_grasp: Initial grasp is required to manually reach the pull-tab area or for the robot to interact with the top rim/lid mechanism
use.physical_constraints: Manually grasping requires a pinch grip on the top near the tab or a two-handed cupped grip for the can body

use.description: Transport the drink from the table to a trash bin or recycling station
use.link_to_grasp: Firm lateral grip is required to prevent tipping the can over during the movement trajectory
use.physical_constraints: Robot must maintain a steady vertical axis to prevent liquid spillage if the can was partially consumed

robustness.assumptions: The can can is rigid, the surface is non-adhesive, and there is no internal pressure that could cause deformation
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If the can is not full, slight tilting might be acceptable; if empty, orientation is less critical, but controlling the center of mass remains vital

object_identification.name: Standard slim aluminum beverage can (250ml V Blue energy drink)
object_identification.attributes: Blue, cylindrical, metallic, open top (tab pulled), standing upright, labeled with product information.

grasp.type: Parallel-jaw power grasp or wrap grasp.
grasp.contact_zone: Opposing large patches on the central cylindrical body of the can.
grasp.accessibility: High, object is isolated in the open center of the table.

planning.approach: Horizontal or slightly downward trajectory towards the sides of the can.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Gripper jaws aligned vertically, open wider than the can width (~53mm), centered on the can's axis.
planning.grasp_contact: Jaws close simultaneously to apply opposing normal forces until safe friction is achieved.
planning.lift_stabilization: Vertical lift, minimizing lateral acceleration to prevent spilling of liquid content.

use.description: Transporting the can to another location.
use.link_to_grasp: Secure grasp required to maintain integrity, but gentle enough not to deform the thin aluminum especially when partially open.
use.physical_constraints: Maintain upright orientation to prevent spilling from the open top.

use.description: Pouring the content into another container.
use.link_to_grasp: Grasp can remain secure, with the addition of a wrist drop/rotation relative to the target container.
use.physical_constraints: Coordinate rotation with linear positioning over the target container to control the dynamic liquid flow.

robustness.assumptions: Object is non-deformable under nominal grasp force; liquid distribution is known.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Use tactile feedback to detect slipping; use vision to verify liquid level or assume full to be safe; reduce speed in case of unknown parameters.

object_identification.name: energy drink aluminum can (250ml)
object_identification.attributes: smooth cylindrical surface, curved top, pull-tab lid, blue color, metallic texture, height ~14cm, diameter ~6cm

grasp.contact_zone: mid-can body (avoiding tab) or tab edges
grasp.accessibility: moderate (requires stable orientation for consistent grip)

planning.approach: perpendicular from above or side (aligned with can axis)
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: can upright or slightly tilted (<30°)
planning.grasp_contact: fingers wrap ~70% circumference (avoid tab contact)
planning.lift_stabilization: lift from center of mass (mid-height) with slight inward force

use.description: consumption (energy drink)
use.link_to_grasp: can must be held upright to open tab and stabilize during drinking
use.physical_constraints: can must remain upright to prevent spillage; tab must be accessible

use.description: recycling disposal (metal recycling bin)
use.link_to_grasp: can must be lifted cleanly to avoid crushing or damaging tab
use.physical_constraints: grip strength must exceed 10N to prevent deformation during handling

robustness.assumptions: can is unopened, no liquid residue, surface clean and dry
robustness.uncertainty_handling: use force/torque sensing to adjust grip pressure; visual depth estimation for can pose correction

object_identification.name: V Blue Guarana Energy Drink Aluminum Can
object_identification.attributes: 250mL cylindrical glossy blue aluminum beverage can, pull-tab lid, upright orientation, transitions from sealed (first image) to opened (second image), rigid undamaged structure

grasp.contact_zone: Mid-lateral vertical surface of the can, between the top aluminum rim and bottom base, avoiding any minor raised elements on the printed exterior
grasp.accessibility: Fully accessible with no obstacles blocking the can's sides, positioned on an unobstructed office table, allowing the robot to approach from any lateral direction

planning.approach: Move the robotic gripper in a straight horizontal line toward the can's midsection from the nearest unobstructed side, aligning the gripper with the can's vertical axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Orient the gripper jaws parallel to the can's height, spaced 1cm wider than the can's diameter to avoid collision during approach
planning.grasp_contact: Close the gripper jaws gradually, applying uniform firm pressure on the can's opposite lateral surfaces to secure the can without denting the aluminum
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift the can straight upward 15cm off the table, maintaining a vertical orientation to prevent liquid shifting that could cause imbalance

use.description: Deliver the unopened energy drink can to a human user for consumption
use.link_to_grasp: The same cylindrical wrap grasp used to pick up the can is maintained during transport to keep the can stable and prevent accidental opening mid-movement
use.physical_constraints: Keep the can's tilt angle within 10 degrees of vertical to avoid spills, limit transport height to 1.2m to keep the can easily retrievable by a standing or seated user

use.description: Dispose of the empty, used can in a designated recycling bin
use.link_to_grasp: The original mid-lateral wrap grasp remains suitable for the empty rigid can, no grasp adjustments are needed
use.physical_constraints: Confirm the can is fully empty of liquid before transport to avoid spills, position the can fully inside the recycling bin to prevent it from falling out or littering

robustness.assumptions: The can is undamaged and structurally rigid, the table surface is flat and stable, no dynamic obstacles block the robot's path between the can and target use locations
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If initial 3D vision mislocalizes the can, run a secondary high-resolution scan to update the can's coordinates; if the first grasp attempt fails to secure the can, back the gripper up 5cm, adjust jaw spacing, and re-attempt the grasp

object_identification.name: USB flash drive
object_identification.attributes: Small portable storage device with a metal USB-A connector and a compact white plastic body, lying flat on a wooden table.

grasp.contact_zone: Grasp the body near the center or rear plastic shell, avoiding the USB connector and avoiding the printed label area if possible.
grasp.accessibility: Highly accessible on the tabletop with no major occlusion; the object is small and low-profile, so a top-down or shallow lateral approach is suitable.

planning.approach: Move the robot hand above the drive, align the gripper with the long axis of the object, and descend slowly to the center of the body.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Orient the gripper fingers parallel to the drive length, with the opening width slightly larger than the body thickness and the connector facing away from the main contact region.
planning.grasp_contact: Close the fingertips gently on opposite sides of the plastic body to secure it with minimal force, using friction contact rather than squeezing the metal connector.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift vertically a short distance while maintaining a light pinch and keep the wrist level to prevent the small drive from slipping or rotating.

use.description: Insert the USB drive into a computer or device to transfer files.
use.link_to_grasp: The pinch grasp positions the drive so the robot can carry and align the connector accurately with the port.
use.physical_constraints: Requires correct connector orientation, precise insertion alignment, and low lateral force to avoid damaging the port or connector.

use.description: Transport or store digital data offline as a removable memory device.
use.link_to_grasp: A stable grasp allows safe pickup from a table and controlled placement into a storage tray or docking area.
use.physical_constraints: The device is small and easy to drop, so the robot must maintain gentle grip force and avoid twisting impacts.

robustness.assumptions: Assumes the USB drive is rigid, lightweight, and resting on a flat surface with the connector exposed.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If pose is uncertain, use vision to estimate the long-axis orientation, approach from above, and close the gripper slowly with force limits to accommodate the small size and reduce slip risk.

object_identification.name: USB drive
object_identification.attributes: small rectangular device with metal USB connector, white body with printer label, placed on wooden desk

grasp.contact_zone: sides of the white body, avoiding the metal connector
grasp.accessibility: high visibility, stationary position on flat surface

planning.approach: approach from side or slightly above towards center of mass
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: align fingers parallel to the longitudinal axis of the drive
planning.grasp_contact: two fingers press on opposite sides of the white casing
planning.lift_stabilization: maintain vertical orientation or follow the drive's current orientation

use.description: insert into computer USB port
use.link_to_grasp: securely hold to align flat face with port
use.physical_constraints: ensure connector is aligned correctly without jamming

use.description: store on shelf or in file
use.link_to_grasp: steady grip to transport
use.physical_constraints: avoid dropping as the label adds slight fragility to the top

robustness.assumptions: drive is dry and static, label adds slight bulk to top surface
robustness.uncertainty_handling: adjust hand aperture to account for varying label thickness

object_identification.name: USB flash drive
object_identification.attributes: Small, rectangular prism body with a metal USB Type-A connector extending from one end, primarily white and blue with black printed text.

grasp.contact_zone: Opposite sides of the wide, flat plastic body, avoiding the USB connector.
grasp.accessibility: High; the object is on a flat surface, with its largest face exposed to the air. The long edges, suitable for contact with most robotic finger pads, are accessible.

planning.approach: Top-down vertical approach or slightly inclined to clear the USB connector.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the gripper jaws parallel to the long axis of the USB drive.
planning.grasp_contact: Simultaneously apply pressure from both jaws onto the center of the wide flat plastic faces.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift straight up, ensuring no rotation or sliding before sufficient friction is applied.

use.description: Inserting into a computer port for data transfer.
use.link_to_grasp: The grasp must be maintained securely to overcome insertion force, utilizing the grasped body to apply directional pressure.
use.physical_constraints: Rotation of the entire gripper/joint would be necessary to align with a vertically or horizontally oriented USB port. A key pin or the asymmetrical internal structure of the USB plug restricts orientation for insertion.

use.description: Placing it into another storage or sorting location (e.g., a pegboard hole, a bin, another table).
use.link_to_grasp: The pinch grasp facilitates easy release at the target location.
use.physical_constraints: Requires precise translational and rotational control to place accurately, avoiding collisions with the target location or other objects.

robustness.assumptions: The object is resting flat on a level table, is rigid enough to withstand gripper forces, and has sufficient friction available to prevent slipping with a feasible normal force.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Use a force-sensitive gripper to detect contacts that do not match expected forces, indicating potential misalignment, or use visual servoing refined with force feedback to precisely adjust position before full force is applied.

object_identification.name: USB flash drive
object_identification.attributes: small rectangular plastic casing, metallic USB connector, dimensions approximately 50x15x1mm, smooth textured surface, lightweight

grasp.contact_zone: metallic connector end and edge contact points
grasp.accessibility: partially obstructed by surface, requires alignment with flat surface

planning.approach: parallel to surface, 10mm above plane, slightly angled for connector visibility
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: rotated 45 degrees around vertical axis, connector facing downward
planning.grasp_contact: fingers clamp around edges while thumb stabilizes connector base
planning.lift_stabilization: gentle upward force with rotational correction to align with vertical axis

use.description: Data transfer between computers or devices via USB ports
use.link_to_grasp: grasp ensures connector alignment for direct insertion into ports
use.physical_constraints: must avoid bending connector, handle with dry fingers to prevent static damage

use.description: Backup and storage of digital files, documents, or media
use.link_to_grasp: secure grasp prevents accidental drops or connector misalignment during transport
use.physical_constraints: store in protective case when not in use to prevent scratches or debris

robustness.assumptions: object is stationary, surface is flat and non-reflective, lighting allows connector detection
robustness.uncertainty_handling: use depth sensing for precise z-coordinate adjustment, apply force control to avoid connector damage

object_identification.name: USB flash drive
object_identification.attributes: small rectangular portable data storage device, white plastic body with grey end cap, exposed metal USB connector, smooth matte surface, compact lightweight form factor, resting unobstructed on a flat wooden table

grasp.contact_zone: the flat midsection of the USB drive, between the metal USB connector and the grey end cap, avoiding the fragile metal connector pins
grasp.accessibility: fully accessible, no surrounding objects blocking reach, positioned in the open center of a clear table surface

planning.approach: approach the USB drive from a directly overhead position, moving slowly toward the table surface to avoid displacing the small device
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: align the robotic gripper's parallel jaws along the short width of the drive, spacing the jaws slightly wider than the drive's thickness to lower onto it without collision
planning.grasp_contact: close the gripper jaws gradually, applying even, gentle pressure on the drive's midsection to secure a stable hold without damaging the plastic casing
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the drive straight upward 15cm above the table, pause to verify no slippage occurs, confirming the grasp is secure before any subsequent movement

use.description: Transfer digital files between two computing devices by inserting the USB drive into a host device's USB port
use.link_to_grasp: retain the same pinch grasp on the drive's midsection, align the exposed metal USB connector with the target port to prepare for insertion
use.physical_constraints: require precise positional alignment to avoid bending USB connector pins, limit insertion force to prevent damage to either the drive or the host port

use.description: Store offline backup data by saving files to the drive, then stowing it in a secure storage case
use.link_to_grasp: use the same pinch grasp to eject the drive from the host device after file transfer, then reposition the drive to place it into its storage case
use.physical_constraints: ensure the drive is fully detached from the host port before lifting to avoid tearing the port or damaging the connector, maintain gentle grip pressure to prevent dropping the small device during transport

robustness.assumptions: the USB drive is stationary on the flat table, has standard dimensions (≈3cm long, 1cm wide) that fit the gripper's pinch range, the table surface is non-slip, no hidden obstacles block the approach path
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if initial visual position estimation is inaccurate, use visual servoing to adjust the gripper's position before grasping; if the first grasp results in slippage, re-open the gripper, re-align, and re-execute the grasp with slightly increased safe grip pressure

object_identification.name: whiteboard eraser
object_identification.attributes: Small rectangular handheld eraser with a wooden body and dark felt/foam wiping surface on one face; compact, low-profile, lightweight, and suited for sliding along a whiteboard surface.

grasp.contact_zone: Grasp the long wooden side faces near the center or slightly off-center, avoiding the dark wiping surface to preserve cleaning contact and friction.
grasp.accessibility: Highly accessible because the object is isolated on a flat table and has straight edges with enough exposed side area for a two-finger or parallel-jaw grasp.

planning.approach: Approach from above with the gripper aligned to the long axis of the eraser and descend slowly to a pre-contact height just above the side faces.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Orient the gripper jaws parallel to the long edges of the eraser, with the opening wider than the object thickness and the wiping surface facing away from the fingers.
planning.grasp_contact: Close the jaws on the wooden side walls with moderate force until stable contact is achieved; avoid compressing the dark wiping face excessively.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift vertically a short distance while maintaining a firm pinch, then verify no slip or rotation; if needed, slightly increase grip force to stabilize the lightweight object.

use.description: Erase marker writing from a whiteboard.
use.link_to_grasp: The robot lifts the eraser and places the dark wiping face against the board for controlled wiping strokes.
use.physical_constraints: Requires enough normal force to maintain contact without excessive pressure; the wiping surface must stay clean and aligned to the board.

use.description: Move the eraser to a storage tray or handoff location.
use.link_to_grasp: A secure side grasp lets the robot transport the object without touching the wiping face, keeping it ready for use.
use.physical_constraints: Needs stable grasp during motion because the object is light and may rotate or slip if acceleration is too high.

robustness.assumptions: Assume the object is a standard whiteboard eraser with rigid sides, a flat base, and moderate friction between gripper pads and wood.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If pose estimation is uncertain, use a slow top-down approach, re-center with visual feedback, and apply gentle compliance to accommodate small shape or orientation errors.

object_identification.name: whiteboard eraser
object_identification.attributes: small rectangular foam block, dual-sided friction/eraser surfaces, lying flat on wood

grasp.contact_zone: top and bottom flat faces along the long axis
grasp.accessibility: high

planning.approach: vertical approach from above or horizontal from the side
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: align gripper parallel to the eraser's short axis
planning.grasp_contact: clamp gently on both long sides
planning.lift_stabilization: lift vertically to clear the tabletop

use.description: cleaning a whiteboard surface
use.link_to_grasp: secured grip ensures consistent pressure for cleaning
use.physical_constraints: requires motion perpendicular to board surface

use.description: wiping dust from flat wood shelves
use.link_to_grasp: ergonomic orientation allows back-and-forth wiping motion
use.physical_constraints: requires orientation perpendicular to the wiping surface

robustness.assumptions: eraser remains static and lies relatively flat on the surface
robustness.uncertainty_handling: verify full contact before lifting via compliance sensors

object_identification.name: whiteboard eraser
object_identification.attributes: rectangular, block-like, lightweight, wooden top, felt-like dark grey erasing surface, resting on its fabric side on a wooden desk

grasp.contact_zone: the middle sections of the two longest vertical wooden faces to ensure a balance point for stable lifting
grasp.accessibility: the side faces are clear and accessible, with enough space on the desk for a robot's end effector to approach from above or the side

planning.approach: top-down vertical approach or slightly inclined side approach, dependent on robot's kinematic constraints, targeting the central region of the wooden block
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: aligning the gripper's fingers with the longest axis of the eraser, with fingers wide open to straddle the block
planning.grasp_contact: lowering the gripper until fingers are at the middle vertical height of the block, then slowly closing the jaw until initial force is detected on both side faces
planning.lift_stabilization: applying a slight compression force tailored to the friction coefficient of the wood, and then lifting vertically with controlled acceleration

use.description: cleaning a whiteboard
use.link_to_grasp: after lifting, the robot re-orients the eraser to be vertical (felt side out), applies it flat to the board, and moves it in linear or sweeping patterns
use.physical_constraints: requires maintaining dynamic normal force to the board while also applying sideways frictional force to wipe markings, and managing eraser cleanliness or rotational alignment

use.description: relocating the eraser onto a shelf or magnetic holder
use.link_to_grasp: the initial side-pinch grasp is used for the entire transfer mission. the robot uses its motion planners to move the arm along a collision-free path to the destination
use.physical_constraints: requires precise positioning of the object at the pick-up and placement points, especially if placing into a magnetic clip or defined tray

robustness.assumptions: the eraser is not stuck to the table, and its center of mass is close to its geometric center. the felt surface doesn't slip significantly when lifted from the sides
robustness.uncertainty_handling: the system uses vision-based object detection for initial positioning, tactile feedback to confirm grasp presence and force range, and potentially a secondary camera on the arm to refine the final approach in case of placement inaccuracy

object_identification.name: whiteboard eraser
object_identification.attributes: rectangular prism, soft rubber surface, smooth edges, light gray color, flat sides, cylindrical hole in center, dimensions approximately 5x2x1 cm

grasp.contact_zone: flat side edges and cylindrical hole
grasp.accessibility: front-facing, elevated on a flat surface

planning.approach: vertical descent aligned with eraser length
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper fingers parallel to eraser length, offset horizontally
planning.grasp_contact: two-fingered pinch on opposite flat sides with slight inward pressure
planning.lift_stabilization: lift vertically upward with slight rotation to prevent surface drag

use.description: removing marker ink from a whiteboard surface
use.link_to_grasp: grasp ensures firm contact for effective ink removal without slipping
use.physical_constraints: must maintain flat contact with whiteboard while applying downward pressure

use.description: cleaning small electronic surfaces (e.g., touchscreens) of dust or smudges
use.link_to_grasp: soft rubber surface allows gentle abrasion without scratching
use.physical_constraints: requires controlled lateral motion to avoid damaging delicate surfaces

robustness.assumptions: eraser is clean, dry, and positioned on a flat horizontal surface without obstructions
robustness.uncertainty_handling: adjust grip force dynamically via tactile feedback if surface irregularities detected, use visual servoing for precise alignment

object_identification.name: whiteboard eraser
object_identification.attributes: rectangular compact block, light natural wooden base, dark abrasive felt cleaning surface, low mass, unobstructed on a light wooden office desk

grasp.contact_zone: opposing narrow vertical side faces of the eraser's wooden base, avoiding the soft felt cleaning surface to maintain grip integrity
grasp.accessibility: fully accessible, no surrounding obstacles blocking approach from any horizontal direction, positioned on a clear flat desk

planning.approach: descend the robotic gripper vertically from a position 20cm above the eraser, aligning the gripper's finger gap with the eraser's long axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper fingers oriented perpendicular to the eraser's long axis, positioned to clamp around the two short side faces of the rectangular eraser
planning.grasp_contact: close gripper fingers to apply uniform gentle pressure on the wooden side faces, achieving a secure non-slip hold
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the eraser 15cm vertically off the desk surface, maintaining a level orientation to confirm grasp stability before initiating further movement

use.description: Erase dry-erase ink markings from a whiteboard by pressing the eraser's felt surface flat against the whiteboard and moving it across marked areas
use.link_to_grasp: the same precision pinch grasp maintains control to apply consistent even pressure against the whiteboard surface during wiping
use.physical_constraints: must keep the felt surface flush against the whiteboard; avoid excessive grip or wiping force that could damage the whiteboard or dislodge the eraser from the gripper

use.description: Store the whiteboard eraser on a mounted whiteboard accessory tray for future access
use.link_to_grasp: the secure precision grasp allows precise placement of the eraser onto the narrow, shallow storage tray
use.physical_constraints: require sufficient vertical clearance above the tray to lower the eraser; align the eraser's base with the tray surface to prevent it from falling off during placement

robustness.assumptions: the eraser has uniform weight distribution, the wooden base provides sufficient friction to prevent slippage under moderate grip pressure, no hidden obstacles block the grasp or movement path
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if initial grasp alignment is misaligned, the gripper will reposition by 1-2cm to center on the eraser; if minor slippage is detected, increase grip pressure by 10% while capping maximum force to avoid damaging the wooden eraser base

object_identification.name: stirrer
object_identification.attributes: A small flat disposable wooden stirrer, light beige, slender rectangular strip with rounded ends, lying flat on a table and easy to pick up by either end or the middle.

grasp.type: precision pinch grasp or small parallel-jaw grasp
grasp.contact_zone: mid-body along the long edges, or near one rounded end if clearance is better
grasp.accessibility: high; the object is isolated, flat on a clear tabletop, and unobstructed from above

planning.approach: Move the gripper above the stirrer with a vertical approach, centering the fingertips over the chosen grasp point while avoiding pushing the object sideways.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the gripper opening parallel to the stirrer’s long axis, with fingers level and slightly wider than the stirrer thickness.
planning.grasp_contact: Close the fingertips gently onto the long edges at the midpoint or one end, applying enough force to secure the thin wooden strip without bending it.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift slowly straight upward, keeping the gripper aligned with the stirrer’s length to prevent rotation, slip, or flexing.

use.description: Stir a drink such as coffee, tea, or a mixed beverage.
use.link_to_grasp: The robot can hold the stirrer near one end to insert the opposite end into the liquid and move it in small circular motions.
use.physical_constraints: The stirrer is thin and disposable, so the robot must avoid excessive force that could snap it or contaminate the beverage.

use.description: Use as a simple applicator or mixing stick for small amounts of glue, paint, or lab liquid.
use.link_to_grasp: A stable pinch grasp near the center lets the robot control the tool tip precisely for spreading or mixing.
use.physical_constraints: The object is lightweight and flexible, so motions should be slow and low-torque to prevent bending or breakage.

robustness.assumptions: The object is a standard wooden stirrer with sufficient rigidity for light manipulation and the surface is dry and non-slippery.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If exact pose is uncertain, the robot should use visual servoing to refine alignment, grasp from the clearest visible end or center, and reattempt with reduced grip force if slip is detected.

object_identification.name: Stirrer
object_identification.attributes: White, slender, flat, wooden stick; approximately 15-20cm length; rest on a flat wooden desk surface.

grasp.contact_zone: Horizontal contact across the center of the stirrer's body
grasp.accessibility: Highly accessible from all sides on a flat surface.

planning.approach: Approach horizontally relative to the desk surface from a position parallel to the stirrer
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Gripper fingers should open broadly to accommodate the slender profile
planning.grasp_contact: Wrap around the center section perpendicular to the stirrer's long axis
planning.lift_stabilization: Maintain a slight overhang and keep gripper closed to prevent slipping, lift vertically along the surface normal.

use.description: Stirring liquids in a cup, potentially using a secondary tool or a specialized end-effector extension.
use.link_to_grasp: The initial lateral pinch is ideal for transport; a specific actuator could then engage the distal tip.
use.physical_constraints: Must overcome torque from viscous resistance; tip interaction is complex.

use.description: Act as a fusee or lever in a simple mechanical linkage designed for lifting tasks.
use.link_to_grasp: Center longitudinal grasp provides an adequate pivot point or load bearing location.
use.physical_constraints: Moment arm and shear strength limits apply under load.

robustness.assumptions: The grasp point center is visually aligned with the object's centroid to minimize tipping moments.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Visual feedback loop is required to ensure object orientation is maintained during vertical extraction from the surface.

object_identification.name: wooden coffee stirrer
object_identification.attributes: thin, flat, semi-rigid, lightweight, light brown, uniform width, rounded ends

grasp.contact_zone: The central section along the flat surfaces of the thin edges, maximizing contact area for stability.
grasp.accessibility: Fully accessible. The object lies flat on the table, providing a stable, unobstructed surface for the gripper to approach and engage.

planning.approach: Direct overhead (top-down) vertical approach to the center of the stirrer.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Rotate the end-effector to align the two parallel gripping surfaces with the long axis of the stirrer's thin edge.
planning.grasp_contact: Simultaneous, gentle contact by both gripper jaws onto the opposite flat surfaces of the stirrer, centered along its length. Apply low, controlled force to avoid damaging the brittle material.
planning.lift_stabilization: Perform a smooth, vertical lift while maintaining a constant, low-pressure grip to ensure the object does not slip or bend out of geometric alignment. Verify the grasp is perpendicular relative to the table surface.

use.description: Stirring a liquid (e.g., coffee) in a cup to mix in sugar and milk.
use.link_to_grasp: This precise horizontal pinch grasp secure holds the midpoint of the stirrer. The ungrasped ends are then available and can be inserted vertically into the liquid for the stirring motion.
use.physical_constraints: The stirrer's vertical alignment must be consistent to enter the cup without colliding with the rim.

use.description: Precisely placing the stirrer into an designated empty holder for disposal or new allocation.
use.link_to_grasp: The precise pinch grasp at the midpoint provides control over parallel transfer. Once transport is complete, a downward motion pushes the stirrer into the vertical slot of the holder.
use.physical_constraints: Requires highly precise alignment between the stirrer's end tip and the small, narrow slot opening of the holder.

robustness.assumptions: The object is assumed to be rigid enough to resist bending from the initial pinch force and that its mass is light enough not to require significant gripping friction. The environment is assumed to be friction-stable.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If resistance is felt during the lift, indicating the stirrer may be stuck or slightly adhered to the surface, the robot will execute a minor, localized lateral vibration or a gentle twist to break adherence before completing the lift. Force feedback sensors will initiate this response pattern.

object_identification.name: stirrer
object_identification.attributes: long slender rod, smooth surface, flexible, typically plastic or metal, ~20cm length, thin width (~5mm)

grasp.contact_zone: flat end face and central shaft
grasp.accessibility: easy, requires clear line of sight to tip and end

planning.approach: vertical alignment from above parallel to the surface
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper fingers open, aligned perpendicular to the rod’s long axis
planning.grasp_contact: fingers close around the end face and shaft (3-4cm from tip)
planning.lift_stabilization: gentle upward lift with slight rotation to prevent slippage

use.description: mixing liquids in containers (e.g., beverages, sauces)
use.link_to_grasp: grasp end allows rotational control for stirring motion
use.physical_constraints: must avoid sharp edges, handle liquids without spillage, limit force to prevent breaking

use.description: cleaning narrow containers or crevices (e.g., petri dishes, test tubes)
use.link_to_grasp: slender form enables access to confined spaces, grasp end provides leverage
use.physical_constraints: must prevent bending/flexing during cleaning, ensure dry before storage

robustness.assumptions: object is clean, dry, and fully visible; environment is clutter-free
robustness.uncertainty_handling: use force control to avoid damage; adjust approach if surface is uneven; verify material before grasping

object_identification.name: Wooden disposable stirrer (popsicle stick)
object_identification.attributes: lightweight, thin, smooth natural wood construction, elongated flat shape with rounded ends, small form factor, low mass, single-use rigid design

grasp.contact_zone: The upper third of the stirrer, 1-2cm from one rounded end, leaving the opposite functional mixing end unobstructed
grasp.accessibility: Fully accessible, positioned centrally on an unobstructed flat table surface with no surrounding barriers blocking approach to the object

planning.approach: Move the robotic gripper straight down from a position directly above the stirrer, aligned parallel to the stirrer's long axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Orient gripper jaws perpendicular to the stirrer's length, hovering 2cm above the planned contact zone to ensure precise alignment
planning.grasp_contact: Close gripper jaws to apply consistent light pressure on the top and bottom surfaces of the stirrer, avoiding excessive force that would damage or splinter the wood
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift the stirrer 10cm straight upward from the table, maintaining a level horizontal orientation to prevent the lightweight object from sliding out of the grasp

use.description: Stir hot or cold beverages to mix in additives like sugar, cream, or drink powder evenly
use.link_to_grasp: Retain the same precision pinch grasp on the upper portion of the stirrer, positioning the free lower end into the beverage to perform controlled circular mixing motions
use.physical_constraints: Keep the gripped section of the stirrer above the liquid surface to maintain gripper traction; limit force to avoid bending or breaking the thin wooden stirrer

use.description: Spread soft low-viscosity substances such as frosting, cream cheese, or craft glue across a flat surface
use.link_to_grasp: Maintain the precision pinch grasp on the upper stirrer section, using the flat wide free end of the stirrer to scrape and distribute the target substance
use.physical_constraints: Avoid excessive downward force that would snap the wooden stirrer; only use with soft, low-density substances that do not overload the lightweight grasp

robustness.assumptions: The stirrer is undamaged with no structural cracks; the table surface is flat and stable; the stirrer remains stationary during gripper approach; the wood has sufficient strength to withstand standard grasp and use forces
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If the stirrer shifts during initial approach, pause to re-align the gripper to the stirrer's center before grasping; use a slightly expanded grip span if the stirrer's exact thickness is undetected to avoid fumbling the small, lightweight object
